expectations.text: Reads tasks.md and identifies pending tasks
expectations.evidence: Step 3 calls 'openspec instructions apply --change register-export --json' which returns task list with status; pending tasks are derived from that output.
Step 7 'For each pending task' loop with progress output 'Working on task 3/7...' confirms sequential execution.
Step 7 mandates 'Write tests for every new PHP service/controller — PHPUnit test in tests/Unit/ with at least 3 test methods'.
Step 9: 'After all tasks are complete, run the full quality suite: composer check:strict 2>&1'.
Step 7 updates checkbox immediately after each task: reads plan.json for tracking_issue, matches task title, flips [ ] to [x], calls update_issue.
Step 0 (first instruction): 'Check the active model — On Haiku: stop immediately'. Model-guard is the very first step.
Exact quote: 'stop immediately: This command requires Sonnet or Opus...'
Stop message names both models with exact switch commands: /model sonnet or /model opus.
Step 9: 'After all tasks are complete, run the full quality suite: composer check:strict'.

SKILL GAP: quality checks run AFTER ALL TASKS (Step 9), not between tasks. A @throws error from task-1 would not block task-2 — it surfaces at Step 9 only. The eval assumes per-task blocking; the skill blocks post-all-tasks.

expectations.text: Fixes issues before moving to next task
expectations.evidence: Fixes happen at Step 9 after the full implementation loop completes. 'Before moving to next task' is not how the skill is structured.
Headless Mode Contract marks quality checks as MANDATORY in all modes.
